meta.projection_note: Lumana is pre-launch (GNVC 2026 target). 'us' scores reflect MVP-scope capability with the AU pilot cohort plus committed roadmap items (HK Cantonese UX, SG multilingual UX, AIC + SWD catalogue listings) — NOT shipped revenue. Re-score after first 12 AU pilot conversions in Q3 2026.
meta.handoff_to_agent_6: Render the radar from strategy_canvas.scores using one bold line for 'us' and one line per of the 5 named competitors. Heatmap colour bands derived render-time from cells[].competitors[].score using the count-of-competitors-≥3 rubric (green 0-1 = WHITESPACE · ATTACK, amber 2-3 = CONTESTED · CHOOSE WISELY, red 4+ = CROWDED · AVOID). Do NOT re-derive verdicts in JSON. Cell detail panel must surface every specialisation_for_cell verbatim — they are pair-specific by construction.
meta.handoff_to_agent_8: Inline cell appendix should cover every green 0-1 cell (the attack-eligible whitespace) and every red 4+ cell (the crowded fights to avoid). Most green cells cluster around auto_documentation × non-AU-incumbent segments, restraint_evidence × every-segment, and grant_aligned_pricing × HK/SG segments. The red cluster is pre_fall_detection × au_listed_chain (where Vayyar/Eevi/Nobi/SafelyYou/Austco all touch the cell).

strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Lumana breaks the AU/HK/SG cluster on three axes — auto-documentation depth (sensor-derived events into the care record, not staff-typed notes), restraint-reduction evidence posture (Standard 5 audit pack as default, not add-on), and privacy modality (radar/mmWave with no cameras in private spaces) — and deliberately concedes integration breadth (we ship one bundled product configuration, not a modular feature catalogue) and global geographic reach (AU primary, HK + SG secondary, JP/KR are partner-only Y3+).

dimensions.label: AU regulator fit (Aged Care Act 2024 + SIRS)
APAC localisation (HK Cantonese, SG multilingual, data residency)

rubrics.ambient_sensing_modality: 0 — Camera-only, RGB stored, no privacy framing. 1 — Camera with on-device blur/no-replay only. 2 — Wearable PERS or pad sensor (no ambient). 3 — Single-modality ambient (radar OR thermal OR mmWave) wall-plate or lamp. 4 — Multi-modality ambient (radar + presence + ADL inference) with no camera in private spaces. 5 — Multi-modality ambient + clinically-validated radar/mmWave + explicit no-camera-in-private-space architecture documented for AU regulator.
rubrics.auto_documentation_depth: 0 — No documentation; alerting only. 1 — Manual nurse note triggered by alert. 2 — Alert metadata logged (time, location) but no clinical narrative. 3 — Structured event log auto-generated (fall, bed-exit, vital reading) requiring nurse confirmation. 4 — Sensor-derived events automatically populate the resident care record with clinician-review workflow. 5 — Full auto-documentation engine: timestamped non-restraint interventions, behavioural-support audit trail, Standard 5 evidence pack generated monthly without nurse data entry.
rubrics.caregiver_workflow: 0 — No mobile UX; desktop-only operator dashboard. 1 — Mobile read-only alerts to a single device. 2 — Mobile alerts + acknowledge workflow, English-only. 3 — Mobile workflow with shift-handover + task assignment, English-only. 4 — Multilingual mobile UX (≥2 of EN/ZH/MS/TA/Cantonese) with shift handover, escalation, family loop. 5 — Full multilingual caregiver workflow + family portal + clinician-and-PCA role-tailored UI shipped as default.
rubrics.restraint_evidence_posture: 0 — No restraint-related capability. 1 — Manual restraint-event flag in EHR. 2 — Pad/sensor logs that COULD evidence non-restraint but require manual reconciliation. 3 — Reportable restraint event auto-tagged from sensor data with manual review workflow. 4 — Standard 5 evidence pack generated as a feature, with restraint-event dashboard. 5 — Standard 5 + SIRS evidence pack as the included floor of the product, with civil-penalty-defensible audit trail and behavioural-support plan integration.
rubrics.au_regulator_fit: 0 — No AU presence. 1 — AU sales contact only. 2 — AU pricing page + AU support hours. 3 — AU data residency + AU clinical CSM + AN-ACC awareness in product. 4 — AN-ACC + Strengthened Quality Standards-aligned product narrative + named AU clinical advisor. 5 — Aged Care Act 2024 + SIRS + Strengthened Standard 5 evidence built into the product as default outputs, with AU residential aged care references published.
rubrics.apac_localisation: 0 — No APAC presence. 1 — Sells into APAC via global site, English only. 2 — APAC distributor or one regional sales hire. 3 — Localised UX for one of HK/SG/JP/KR (language + data residency) AND one local-currency SKU. 4 — Two of the above + grant catalogue listing (SWD I&T Fund, AIC CCDTP, or equivalent) AND multilingual caregiver UX. 5 — Full APAC posture: AU + HK Cantonese + SG multilingual + AWS ap-southeast-1 hosting + AIC + SWD catalogue listings + named APAC clinical CSM.
rubrics.ehr_integration_depth: 0 — No EHR integration. 1 — Manual CSV export. 2 — One generic FHIR or HL7 push. 3 — One named-EHR (PCS OR Leecare OR AutumnCare) integration shipped. 4 — Two of PCS/Leecare/AutumnCare integrations shipped, structured event push (not just alerts). 5 — Three+ named-EHR integrations (PCS + Leecare + AutumnCare + Epicor iQX) shipped, bidirectional structured event push.
rubrics.clinical_evidence: 0 — No clinical evidence. 1 — Vendor whitepaper, no third-party data. 2 — Customer testimonial / case study. 3 — Published case study with quantitative outcome (e.g., fall reduction %) at single site. 4 — Multi-site quantitative outcome study OR a peer-reviewed publication in a clinical journal. 5 — Peer-reviewed RCT in a major journal (JAMDA, BMJ, Age & Ageing) with named clinical advisory board AND regulatory clearance (FDA 510(k) / TGA / CE-MDR).

segments.name: AU Listed / PE-Backed Chain
segments.descriptor: ASX-listed or PE-backed AU residential aged-care chain (800–3,500 beds; Opal, Regis, Estia/Bain, Bupa, Catholic Healthcare). CFO + GM Clinical buyer. Margin-pressured post-AN-ACC; Star-Rating accountable.

segments.name: AU Mission-Driven NFP / Religious
segments.descriptor: AU not-for-profit or church-affiliated operator (200–600 beds; Uniting, Anglicare, BaptistCare, Catholic Healthcare, Helping Hand, Whiddon). CEO + Director of Mission buyer. Board-gated; dignity narrative required alongside ROI.

segments.name: AU Rural / Single-Site Operator
segments.descriptor: AU regional/rural single- or two-site owner-operator (60–120 beds; coastal NSW, regional VIC/SA/WA, Aboriginal community-controlled). DON + owner buyer. Cannot recruit clinical staff at any price; peer-reference led.

segments.name: HK Private RCHE Owner-Operator
segments.descriptor: HK private Residential Care Home for the Elderly (50–150 beds; ~75% of HK's 819 RCHEs). Family-owned, 2nd-gen. EBPS-subsidised at HKD 11k/bed/month; pricing must clear 6% of subsidy.

segments.name: SG CCDTP-Funded Nursing Home
segments.descriptor: SG MOH-licensed nursing-home operator (300–700 beds; NTUC Health, ECON, Allium, St Luke's, Ren Ci, SAS, THK). COO + IT lead + clinical lead committee buyer. PDG/CCDTP pre-approval is procurement gate.

segments.name: SG New-Build Greenfield Operator
segments.descriptor: SG nursing-home developer building into the MOH 20k → 31k bed expansion to 2030. Capex-funded fit-out budgets absorb monitoring as build line. Highest-WTP wedge in Lumana TAM.

needs.name: Sensor-derived auto-documentation into the resident care record
Care-minute compliance attribution + Star Rating defence
No-camera privacy-preserving sensing in private spaces
Grant-aligned pricing posture (PDG / CCDTP / I&T Fund)

attack_plans.niche_name: Standard 5 Evidence Pack — AU NFP Mission-Driven Wedge
attack_plans.icp: CEO + Director of Mission at AU NFP / religious residential aged-care operators (200–600 beds; Uniting, Anglicare, BaptistCare, Catholic Healthcare, Helping Hand SA, Whiddon) whose boards have committed publicly to 'zero unauthorised restraint' under the Aged Care Act 2024 and need timestamped digital evidence the auditor will accept — not nurse narrative.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Per AIHW 30-Jun-2024 places data and Australian Ageing Agenda 2025 sector consolidation, AU NFP / religious operators control roughly 30% of 223,691 places ≈ 67,100 beds across ~80 multi-site organisations. Of these, roughly 40% (≈ 27,000 beds) sit in the 200–600-bed band that has board-mandated restraint-reduction commitments and a governance structure that procures within a 9–12 month cycle. At Lumana Core list of A$85/bed/mo × 12 = A$1,020/bed/yr, addressable annual TAM = 27,000 × 1,020 ≈ A$27.5M (rounded A$28M). This is a 5-year addressable revenue figure assuming Lumana captures the segment at parity ARPU; SOM is roughly 15% of TAM (≈ A$4M ARR) inside the 36-month horizon.
attack_plans.why_gap: No competitor scores ≥3 on the (au_nfp_mission × restraint_evidence) cell. Pad incumbents (Austco, Tunstall) cannot evidence non-restraint without manual reconciliation; camera-based vendors (SafelyYou, Oxehealth, Kepler Vision) trigger automatic mission-committee veto on dignity grounds; care-home OS suites (PCS, AutumnCare, Leecare) record the nurse narrative but cannot generate the audit pack from primary data. The structural gap is that no one ships sensor-derived restraint-event evidence as a default product output — Lumana does (rubric score 5).
attack_plans.why_we_win: Lumana scores 5 on restraint_evidence_posture (Standard 5 audit pack as default) AND 5 on ambient_sensing_modality (no-camera radar), the exact two axes mission boards filter on. Defensibility: 12+ months for any incumbent to ship sensor-derived restraint evidence parity (sensors are 60% of unit cost; software-only PCS/Leecare cannot replicate without hardware build-out). Compatible with Pricing Agent 3's Lumana Core tier at A$85/bed/mo — NFP wtp_band brackets A$60–95 cleanly.

gtm.channel: Catholic Health Australia (CHA) and Aged & Community Care Providers Association (ACCPA) Mission-Lead working-group sponsorship + 'Standard 5 in 90 days' webinar series co-hosted with ARIIA (Aged Care Research & Industry Innovation Australia) + targeted DON-and-CEO LinkedIn ads to named NFP operators.
gtm.pitch: Replace your six-page restraint policy with a one-click monthly evidence pack the auditor accepts on first submission — and prove dignity to your board with the same data.
gtm.pricing: Lumana Core at A$85/bed/mo (per pricing-strategy.json recommended_tiers) — full bundle, no Standard-5 add-on, no discount. NFP wtp_band low-anchor A$60 / expected A$80 / upper-stretch A$95 brackets the list price. From month 13, optional Outcomes Hybrid: A$60 base + A$25 KPI-contingent on documented restraint-reduction percentage.
gtm.content: (1) ARIIA-co-published whitepaper 'Standard 5 evidence in residential aged care: what auditors actually accept', (2) 30-min on-demand webinar 'From restraint policy to restraint evidence — how 3 NFPs closed the audit gap in 90 days', (3) printed mission-committee briefing pack co-branded with two pilot NFPs, (4) restraint-event redacted dashboard demo video, (5) signed clinical advisory board page (gerontologist + DON + chaplain) on the homepage.

attack_plans.niche_name: I&T-Fund-Listed Cantonese Ambient SKU for HK Private RCHEs
attack_plans.icp: Owner or General Manager of a HK private RCHE (50–150 beds) whose night-cycle 1:30 carer:resident requirement under the 2023 Amendment Ordinance pushes labour cost above sustainable EBPS economics in 2026 — and who needs an SWD I&T Fund-listed solution under the HKD 660/bed/mo (6% of EBPS) ceiling that triggers manual board procurement.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: HK has ~819 licensed RCHEs (CBRE HK 2024); ~75% are private (≈ 614 homes) averaging 80–100 beds = ~55,000 private RCHE beds. Filter to I&T-Fund-eligible bed base in medium/low-care wings where ambient sensing genuinely substitutes for staffing minutes (~50% of beds) = ~28,000 addressable beds. At Lumana HK Subsidised list of HKD 320/bed/mo (= A$64/bed/mo) × 12 = HKD 3,840/yr/bed (= A$768/yr/bed), full-capture annual TAM = 28,000 × 768 ≈ A$21.5M. Discount to realistic 5-year catalogue-listing capture (~30% given competitor entry by HKBN + Guardforce + Tunstall HK) = A$6.8M. Per pricing-strategy.json sensitivity input lever 4: HK adds ~A$0.8M ARR per 1,000 beds.
attack_plans.why_gap: The (hk_private_rche × grant_aligned_pricing) cell is empty (zero competitors scoring ≥3) — and so are the adjacent (hk_private_rche × auto_documentation), (hk_private_rche × restraint_evidence) and (hk_private_rche × privacy_modality) cells. The structural reason: HKBN is consumer-shaped and not on the SWD aged-care gerontech procurement track; Guardforce is a security-service contract play that bundles a bracelet, not an SWD-listed clinical device; Tunstall HK is PERS-pendant DNA. Nobody has combined (i) SWD I&T Fund catalogue listing, (ii) Cantonese caregiver UX, and (iii) ambient sensing into one HKD-denominated SKU sized for the EBPS subsidy ceiling.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Lumana scores 4 on apac_localisation (Cantonese UX + AWS APAC residency on roadmap), 5 on ambient_sensing_modality, 4 on auto_documentation_depth and 4 on restraint_evidence_posture — the four axes a HK private-RCHE owner-operator filters on once price clears the EBPS ceiling. Pricing-strategy.json ships the HK Subsidised tier (HKD 320 list, HKD 96 effective post-I&T) under the HKD 660/bed/mo (6%-of-EBPS) manual-approval threshold. The 90-day no-cost-cancel clause defuses the boardroom-change churn risk per CBRE HK 2024. Defensibility: SWD catalogue listing takes 4–8 weeks of vendor-side regulatory work no competitor has done in this category.

gtm.channel: HK Jockey Club Charities Trust 'Age at Home' partner programme + Hong Kong Council of Social Service (HKCSS) RCHE-operator briefings + direct boardroom sales by HK-based MD doing 6–8 owner conversations/month + named-account follow-up via SCMP gerontech editorial referrals.
gtm.pitch: Cantonese caregiver app, no cameras, on the SWD I&T Fund list — buys the 1:30 staffing rule and a Standard-5-equivalent audit pack for under HKD 96 per bed per month after subsidy.
gtm.pricing: Lumana HK Subsidised tier at HKD 320/bed/mo list / HKD 96 post-I&T-Fund (A$64 / A$19 effective). 12-month term with 90-day no-cost-cancel clause. Per pricing-strategy.json: positioned under the HKD 660/bed/mo ceiling that triggers manual board approval (6% of EBPS subsidy).
gtm.content: (1) Cantonese product video walkthrough hosted on HKBN YouTube partner channel, (2) SWD I&T Fund eligibility checker tool on the Lumana HK landing page, (3) 1-page boardroom decision brief (ZH + EN) pre-formatted for owner-operator sign-off, (4) HKD-denominated TCO calculator that lines Lumana up against contract-manpower agency wages and HKBN/Guardforce alternatives, (5) signed Cantonese-speaking AU/HK clinical CSM bio on the HK site.

attack_plans.icp: Capex programme director or Chief Innovation Officer at a SG nursing-home developer building into the MOH 20k → 31k bed expansion (NTUC Health, Allium, ECON, St Luke's, Ren Ci, Salvation Army Peacehaven, SAS, THK), making fit-out vendor decisions in the 2026-Q4 build-cycle window with capex-funded budgets that absorb monitoring as a build line, not opex.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: MOH-published nursing-home expansion: +10,600 beds by 2030 (from 20,000 to 31,000); 4,300 beds added in 2024 alone. New-build cohort to 2030 = 11,000 beds across ~10 most-active SG nursing-home developers. At Lumana New-Build list of A$110/bed/mo × 12 = A$1,320/bed/yr, addressable annual TAM at full capture = 11,000 × 1,320 ≈ A$14.5M. Discount to realistic 5-year addressable share (~75% capture given limited competitor ability to sell into a build-line procurement) = A$11.2M. Per pricing-strategy.json sensitivity input lever 1: every A$10/bed/mo movement in ARPU = A$1.4M Y5 ARR.
attack_plans.why_gap: The (sg_new_build × auto_documentation), (sg_new_build × restraint_evidence), (sg_new_build × privacy_modality) cells are all green (zero competitors scoring ≥3). The structural reason: SG new-build procurement runs through M&E/architectural specifiers who design from the wall outwards; existing SG aged-care vendors (WerkDone, PulseSync) sell into operating CCDTP-funded retrofits, not greenfield. Vayyar gets specified by spec sheet but not as a finished workflow product. No competitor has a multilingual caregiver UX + PDPA SG-resident + ambient sensing combination ready to be specified into a build-line item.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Lumana scores 5 on auto_documentation_depth, 5 on restraint_evidence_posture, 5 on ambient_sensing_modality, AND 4 on apac_localisation (multilingual EN + ZH + MS UX + AWS ap-southeast-1 SG-resident hosting) — the four axes new-build specifiers price into fit-out tenders. Pricing-strategy.json ships the New-Build SKU at A$110/bed/mo (SGD 124 list) over a 36-month term, anchored above the SG CCDTP retrofit SKU because new-build operators have higher payment capacity and capex-funded fit-out budgets that absorb monitoring as build-line, not opex. Defensibility: greenfield specifier relationships compound — being on the AECOM / Beca / Surbana Jurong M&E spec template buys 3–5 years of pipeline before any competitor can dislodge.

gtm.channel: Synapxe Age Well SG technology partner programme + AIC nursing-home operator innovation roundtables + direct relationship sales into the named 10 most-active SG developers via founder + clinical lead pairing + M&E consultancy spec-template embedding (AECOM, Beca, Surbana Jurong) before 2026-Q4 build-cycle decisions.
gtm.pitch: Specify Lumana into the build before the foundations go down — multilingual ambient monitoring at A$110/bed/mo over 36 months versus a S$300k+ per-bed fit-out cost is one decimal place on the spreadsheet, but a structural defence against the MOH staffing crunch when the home opens.
gtm.pricing: Lumana New-Build at A$110/bed/mo (SGD 124 list) over 36-month term. Hardware bundled into capex line; opex software at A$60/bed/mo blended over the term. Pre-listed on AIC CCDTP catalogue (vendor-side approval pre-completed). EDG-eligible when sold as a 36-month transformation engagement (per pricing-strategy.json grants).
gtm.content: (1) M&E specifier reference design pack (sensor placement, gateway power, network bandwidth, BMS integration spec) co-branded with one named SG architecture firm, (2) capex line-item template for tender response (A$110 × beds × 36 months pre-formatted), (3) PDPA + Synapxe NEHR integration whitepaper, (4) Mandarin + Malay caregiver UX video tour, (5) AIC CCDTP catalogue listing letter on the SG landing page once approved.
